description: Four consecutive attempts fail with the SAME fingerprint (the real underlying bug is never fixed — only the surrounding strategy changes). At k=3 this crosses the stall threshold, and because the streak (4) is >= k+1 the verdict escalates rather than just recommending a strategy switch. One action ('retry network call') was tried twice under this fingerprint — a dead end the next turn must avoid.
harness: scripts/loop_journal.py:analyze(rows, k) — the same pure function hooks/loop_stop.py and the hierarchical planner call; no subprocess needed since it is side-effect-free.
notes: STALLED does not by itself stop the loop (hooks/loop_stop.py never calls analyze()) — it is the signal the hierarchical planner (scripts/hierarchical_planner.py) and the agent's own per-turn read (`loop_journal.py resume`) use to move phase to `escalate` (zero further mutations, gather context for a human handoff) instead of continuing to oscillate.

switch-strategy vs escalate is threshold-sensitive: streak==k recommends switch-strategy, streak>=k+1 recommends escalate. This fixture is deliberately at streak=4 (k+1) to exercise the escalate branch, not just the stall branch.
